testCases.scenario: AC1: Fill signup form and register — POSITIVE (happy path)
testCases.testCase: Complete the 2-step signup with valid data; server accepts the registration
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; step-1 'Register Your Restaurant' form is visible
testSteps: Fill Restaurant Name with a unique timestamp-suffixed value
Fill Full Name, Business Email, Phone Number with valid values
Check 'I agree to the Terms and Conditions'
testCases.testData:
Restaurant: MoonTest<ts>
Full Name: Arslan Tester
Email: arslan.moon+ac1<ts>@yopmail.com
Phone: 5555550199
Password: TestPass!2025
testCases.expectedResult: Server accepts registration; URL leaves /signup (typically navigates to /select-location)
testCases.postCondition: New tenant created on server; user can proceed to choose a location
testCases.linkedTestTitle: AC1 — fills both steps and the app accepts the registration (URL leaves /signup)

testCases.scenario: AC2: Verify the user is registered — POSITIVE
testCases.testCase: After Create Account, three independent post-registration signals all hold
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; valid registration just submitted
testSteps: Complete the full 2-step signup with valid data
Verify the 'Select Your Location' heading is visible
Verify the 'Restaurant: <name>' paragraph references the just-created restaurant
testCases.testData:
Restaurant: MoonVerify<ts>
Email: arslan.moon+ac2<ts>@yopmail.com
Password: TestPass!2025
testCases.expectedResult: All three signals visible simultaneously: URL = /select-location, heading visible, restaurant paragraph confirms the new account
testCases.linkedTestTitle: AC2 — after Create Account, the app surfaces the post-registration screen referencing the new restaurant

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with Restaurant Name empty
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; step-1 form visible
testSteps: Fill all other required step-1 fields with valid values
testCases.testData:
Restaurant Name: (empty)
Full Name: Arslan
Email: arslan.moon+rn@yopmail.com
Phone: 5555550199
testCases.expectedResult: 'Next' is disabled OR a validation message appears under Restaurant Name; form does not advance to step 2

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with Full Name empty
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; step-1 form visible
testSteps: Fill Restaurant Name; leave Full Name empty
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message appears; form does not advance

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with Business Email empty
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; step-1 form visible
testSteps: Fill all step-1 fields except Business Email
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message appears under Business Email; form does not advance

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with a Business Email that has no '@' symbol
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message: 'Please enter a valid email address'; form does not advance

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with letters in the Phone Number field
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message rejects the input OR the field strips non-digits; form does not advance if invalid

testCases.testCase: Submit step 1 with an email already registered in the system
testSteps: Fill all step-1 fields, using the already-registered email
Click 'Next' (step 2 may appear depending on when the uniqueness check fires)
Complete step 2 if reached and click 'Create Account'
testCases.expectedResult: Server rejects with a message such as 'Email already in use'; URL stays on /signup; no new tenant created
testCases.postCondition: No duplicate account; existing account untouched

testCases.testCase: Subdomain is read-only and auto-derived from Restaurant Name
testSteps: Inspect the Subdomain input; confirm it has the readonly attribute
testCases.expectedResult: Subdomain becomes 'myeatery42' (lowercased, alphanumeric only); user cannot edit it directly

testCases.testCase: Step 2 rejects mismatching Password and Confirm Password values
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message: 'Passwords do not match'; Create Account is disabled OR submission is blocked

testCases.testCase: Step 2 enforces a minimum password length
testSteps: Enter a short password (e.g., 'abc') in both Password and Confirm Password
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message: minimum-length requirement is not met; submission is blocked

testCases.testCase: Create Account is disabled until 'I agree to the Terms and Conditions' is checked
testCases.preCondition: Step 2 visible; passwords filled with matching valid values
testSteps: Fill Password and Confirm Password with valid matching values
Observe the Create Account button state
testCases.expectedResult: Create Account button is disabled; cannot be clicked

testCases.testCase: Subdomain, Location Name, and Address fields are read-only and cannot be edited by clicking
testSteps: Confirm each of #subDomain, #locationName, #locationAddress has the readonly attribute
Click into each and attempt to type
testCases.expectedResult: None of the three fields accept keyboard input; the readonly attribute is set on the input elements

testCases.testCase: Next button reflects validity of step-1 required fields in real time
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /signup; form is empty
testSteps: Observe the Next button state when the form is empty
Observe the Next button state at each step
testCases.expectedResult: Next button is disabled when any required field is empty/invalid; becomes enabled once all required fields have valid values

testCases.testCase: Both Password and Confirm Password fields have a working show/hide toggle
testSteps: Click the 'Show password' button next to Password
testCases.expectedResult: Each show-password button toggles the corresponding field between type='password' and type='text'; mask state is independent for the two fields

testCases.scenario: Step 2 — Back button preserves step-1 data
testCases.testCase: Clicking Back from step 2 returns to step 1 with the previously entered values intact
testCases.preCondition: Step 1 was filled and Next clicked; user is on step 2
testCases.expectedResult: Step 1 reappears; all editable fields retain the values entered previously (no data loss)
testCases.postCondition: User can navigate back and forth between steps without losing input

testCases.testCase: 'Sign in' link on /signup navigates to /login
testSteps: Click the 'Sign in' link below the form
testCases.expectedResult: Browser navigates to /login

testCases.testCase: '← Back' link navigates to /
testCases.expectedResult: Browser navigates to the homepage

testCases.testCase: Restaurant Name with leading/trailing whitespace
testSteps: Enter '   My Eatery   ' in Restaurant Name
Complete the rest of step 1 and proceed
testCases.expectedResult: App trims whitespace before deriving the subdomain; subdomain is 'myeatery' not 'my-eatery'

testCases.testCase: Restaurant Name with special characters
testSteps: Enter 'My Eatery & Café (2026)!' in Restaurant Name
testCases.expectedResult: Subdomain is derived to a URL-safe slug (alphanumeric + dashes only): e.g., 'myeaterycafe2026'; no special characters leak into the subdomain

testCases.testCase: Disposable email (yopmail) is accepted (or rejected with clear message)
testCases.expectedResult: App either (a) accepts the email and proceeds with signup, or (b) explicitly rejects with 'Disposable email addresses are not allowed'. Silent failure is not acceptable.

testCases.testCase: Subdomain collision — Restaurant Name yields a subdomain that already exists
testCases.preCondition: An account 'demo' already exists; new user tries Restaurant Name 'Demo'
testSteps: Enter 'Demo' as Restaurant Name (auto-derives to existing subdomain 'demo')
Complete the full form and click Create Account
testCases.testData: Restaurant Name: Demo (collides with existing tenant)
testCases.expectedResult: Server rejects the registration with a clear message: 'Subdomain already in use' or similar; URL stays on /signup

testCases.testCase: Password fields use type='password' (masked, not in DOM as plaintext)
testSteps: Inspect both Password and Confirm Password inputs in DevTools
testCases.expectedResult: Both inputs default to type='password'; values are masked in the DOM by default

testCases.testCase: Form posts over HTTPS (credentials encrypted in transit)
testSteps: Inspect the request URL and protocol
testCases.expectedResult: The form-submit request goes to an https:// endpoint; no plaintext HTTP credentials in transit

testCases.testCase: XSS payload in Restaurant Name is sanitized
testSteps: Complete signup and observe the post-signup screen 'Restaurant: <name>' paragraph
testCases.expectedResult: No JS alert fires; the value is either rejected at input validation OR rendered as escaped text (no HTML injection)

testCases.testCase: Password not echoed in the response or DOM after successful submission
testSteps: After Create Account succeeds, open DevTools
Search the DOM for the password string
Inspect the network response for any password field
testCases.expectedResult: Password string does NOT appear anywhere in the DOM or in any HTTP response body. (Only the hashed/transient form submission contained it.)
